propositions: Add a custom-verifier mode to MCP auth activated by including 'custom' in the mcp.auth_strategies list, loading an operator-supplied module:factory via auth_custom_import.
The factory must return an object implementing the SDK TokenVerifier protocol; loading validates the protocol and fails closed if it is unsatisfied or the import path is unresolvable.
The composite's mandatory audience re-check still applies to custom verifiers, rejecting AccessTokens with resource=None or a wrong resource so a buggy/hostile verifier cannot bypass RFC 8707.
Import resolution uses importlib.import_module and does not insert cwd into sys.path, so a module resolvable only from the current working directory is not loaded.

The auth_custom_import config value is a trusted operator-supplied boundary read only from resolved config at startup, never from a request, and its derived env var must appear in MCP_ENV_VARS and server.json.
The import path executes arbitrary operator-configured code at load time and this trust boundary is documented in docs/mcp-auth.md.
